fields.description:
The current MiniYARNCluster can only support 1 node manager, which is not enough for the full test purposes.

Would like to have a simulator that can support multiple node managers as the real scenario. This might be beneficial for hadoop users, testers and developers.
fields.summary: Multiple node managers support for the MiniYARNCluster

comments.body:
This is just an initial patch. As I am not experienced, I am looking to get some feedback on whether I am headed in the right direction and if so, will add tests and make sure everything else is complete.

Thanks in advance,
Anupam

It looks like a good start.  In the constructor for the YarnMiniMRCluster I would prefer to see more code reuse between the two.  Perhaps if the original constructor could just call the new one with a 1 for the number of clusters.

Also I think you will need to update various configs for the different node managers created.  This is so there are no host/port collisions between the different node managers running on the same box.

I tried to address the ports issue in my revised patch (attached) by using port 0, but I am failing unit tests because the NMs fail to start properly. Could someone help debug the problem I am running into? Also attaching the log for this failure.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Test set: org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.TestContainerTokenSecretManager
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Tests run: 1, Failures: 1, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0, Time elapsed: 24.324 sec <<< FAILURE!
test(org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.TestContainerTokenSecretManager)  Time elapsed: 23.654 sec  <<< FAILURE!
junit.framework.AssertionFailedError: expected:<LAUNCHED> but was:<FAILED>
	at junit.framework.Assert.fail(Assert.java:47)
	at junit.framework.Assert.failNotEquals(Assert.java:283)
	at junit.framework.Assert.assertEquals(Assert.java:64)
	at junit.framework.Assert.assertEquals(Assert.java:71)
	at org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.TestContainerTokenSecretManager.test(TestContainerTokenSecretManager.java:188)

Looked at the patch. Mostly looks fine, some minor comments:
 - Disable TestMRJobs.testSleepJobWithSecurityOn(), it won't work on Jenkins.
 - Undo the changes in ShuffleHandler.Shuffle w.r.t port, it was done like that to avoid findBugs warnings.
 - Wrap around lines longer than 80 chars.
 - ContainerManager.start(): Move the log "ContainerManager started at" to the end of the method before super.start(). Similarly move the log message in ResourceLocalizationService.start() to the end.
 - MiniYarnCluster:
   -- You can remove the field nodeManagerWrapper completely
   -- Remove unused imports.

Test patch returns

+1 overall.  

    +1 @author.  The patch does not contain any @author tags.

    +1 tests included.  The patch appears to include 9 new or modified tests.

    +1 javadoc.  The javadoc tool did not generate any warning messages.

    +1 javac.  The applied patch does not increase the total number of javac compiler warnings.

    +1 findbugs.  The patch does not introduce any new Findbugs (version 1.3.9) warnings.

    +1 release audit.  The applied patch does not increase the total number of release audit warnings.
Thanks a lot Vinod! Addressed all your comments and uploaded the new patch. Test patch results are above (submitted that first by mistake).

Looks good.

Jenkins didn't seem to have picked this up. Will reattach and resubmit it again.
Reattaching the same patch.

This patch has bugs which is causing the tests' to hang at integration tests, the first one being TestMROldApiJobs.

Please don't submit this patch to Jenkins, one of the builds is stuck and I'm afraid it is going to fill up the disks. Will see how to stop that.

Anupam, in the mean  while, please run the tests locally with -Dtest.logs=false and try fixing the bug. Thanks!

I was able to reproduce the hanging test and was investigating through the root cause. But, after doing a svn update this morning, all tests pass and there is no longer a hang. I am re-attaching the patch. Looks like someone else checked in a fix for something that was hitting the same bug that my patch was hitting. I have not yet analyzed exactly which change hit the nail.

-1 overall.  Here are the results of testing the latest attachment 
  http://issues.apache.org/jira/secure/attachment/12497506/MAPREDUCE-2986-branch-0_23.patch
  against trunk revision .

    +1 @author.  The patch does not contain any @author tags.

    +1 tests included.  The patch appears to include 9 new or modified tests.

    -1 patch.  The patch command could not apply the patch.

Console output: https://builds.apache.org/job/PreCommit-MAPREDUCE-Build/936//console

This message is automatically generated.
Looks like an upmerge issue caused by another checkin caused the patch application to not succeed. Have resolved the conflicts and uploading new patch after running the tests locally.
Re-attaching to kick Jenkins

The patch still has problems, cancelling for fixing them.

Please run "mvn clean install" before submitting the patch. Thanks.

We are seeing problems in the patch due to the bug described in MAPREDUCE-3159. Upon removing this deletion of the appCache, the patch runs all tests successfully. Waiting for fix to finalize my patch.

Verified that the patches for MAPREDUCE-3159 and MAPREDUCE-3162 applied on top of my patch indeed fix the issue with intermittent failure of the tests. Waiting for them to be resolved and committed before I can make this patch available.

bq. Verified that the patches for MAPREDUCE-3159 and MAPREDUCE-3162 applied on top of my patch indeed fix the issue with intermittent failure of the tests.
That's great news! So your patch was indeed failing because of existing bugs. I'll try and move the blocking tickets forward.

Thanks for being on top of this, Anupam!

Since MAPREDUCE-3159 and MAPREDUCE-3162 have now gone in, I have upmerged and cleaned up this patch.

I have also run the MR test suite multiple times and made sure all tests pass in order to confirm that there has been no regression in the last several days.

Requesting for a final +1 and commit.

-1 overall.  Here are the results of testing the latest attachment 
  http://issues.apache.org/jira/secure/attachment/12500735/MAPREDUCE-2986-branch-0_23.patch
  against trunk revision .

    +1 @author.  The patch does not contain any @author tags.

    -1 core tests.  The patch failed these unit tests:
                  org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.nodemanager.containermanager.localizer.TestResourceLocalizationService

    +1 contrib tests.  The patch passed contrib unit tests.

The javadoc warnings and Findbugs warnings are spurious and have nothing to do with my patch.

The unit test failures are not reproducible on my local machine even after many runs of the test suite - I don't yet understand why it failed on Jenkins.
cancelling and re-submitting to see if Jenkins can reproduce the test failure
